blocks.body: a^2 - b^2 = (a+b)(a-b). Both terms must be perfect squares and there is subtraction. Example: x^2 - 25 = (x+5)(x-5). 4x^2 - 9 = (2x+3)(2x-3).

To check if a trinomial is a perfect square: the first and last terms must be perfect squares, and the middle term must be twice the product of their square roots. x^2 + 6x + 9: sqrt(x^2)=x, sqrt(9)=3, 2(x)(3)=6x. Yes!
